threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still look surprised when the Tube's delayed. Locals just shrug and head for the nearest coffee shop like it's a normal Tuesday.
Last night on the 196, heard a guy singing opera to the driver. He had the whole bus chuckling by the end. Only in Brixton!

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I found myself in line behind an old man arguing with the bus driver about his missing discount card. Only in Neukölln, right?

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin do you find a club that opens for breakfast after a night of partying. It's wild to see folks dancing at 8 AM while eating currywurst.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell a local by their reaction to BART delays—while newbies panic, we just grab a burrito and stroll to the nearest bus stop.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 5 this morning, driver stopped for a full minute to coax a pigeon off the tracks. Pigeons really know how to hold up a line!

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists rave about our 'hidden' squares, but miss that locals avoid them during siesta. They’re quiet for a reason after lunch.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell by the way they order coffee—still asking for 'un café con leche' instead of 'un cortado' like a true local.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it 'the Tube' instead of just 'the Overground' for the Dalston Junction line. Classic newbie mistake.
Caught a night bus home from Brixton, and the driver had a full-on rant about how no one appreciates the late-night shift. You could feel the tiredness in the air.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw an artist painting a mural while a group of kids played soccer on the same street—classic Neukölln vibes.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: You ever notice how some U-Bahn stations have their own unique art installations? Like the surreal tiles at Kottbusser Tor—each telling a different story.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know you’re local when you can spot a Muni delay a mile away—which is basically every day now, but especially at Civic Center. Makes you nostalgic for the old days.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 49 stuck at Civic Center today, dog on the tracks. The conductor couldn’t help but mutter, 'Only in SF.'

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think all of Gràcia is gaudy Gaudí. They miss the local bakeries where the croissants have a secret spice mix that nobody talks about.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They’re the ones still looking at a street map instead of just following Google Maps. You can spot them fumbling in front of the Passeig de Gràcia.
